pr.title: Disable `-W all` and fix include paths in `configure.py`
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Small build-configuration cleanup: the PR disabled the Metrowerks `-W all` warning flag, added `-cwd source`, removed the global `-i include` path, and adjusted three baselib source files to include their colocated headers directly. This appears to make include resolution depend less on a broad global include path and more on source-relative lookup. No PR body or review discussion was available, so intent beyond the title and diff is inferred from the changed compiler flags and include edits.
what_changed: In `configure.py`, `-W all` was commented out rather than emitted as an active compiler flag.
In `configure.py`, `-cwd source` was added to the compiler flag list.
In `configure.py`, the broad include path `-i include` was removed, while other include paths such as `-i src/melee`, `-i src/melee/ft/chara`, and `-i src` remained.
`src/sysdolphin/baselib/fobj.c` changed its first include from `#include "baselib/fobj.h"` to `#include "fobj.h"`.
`src/sysdolphin/baselib/lobj.c` changed its first include from `#include "baselib/lobj.h"` to `#include "lobj.h"`.
`src/sysdolphin/baselib/mobj.c` changed its first include from `#include "baselib/mobj.h"` to `#include "mobj.h"`.
smart_moves: The include-path fix was paired with local include spelling changes in the affected baselib C files, avoiding a configure-only change that would break those sources.
Using direct local header includes such as `"fobj.h"`, `"lobj.h"`, and `"mobj.h"` makes these files less dependent on a repository-level `include` search path.
Commenting out `-W all` preserves a visible record of the flag while disabling it, which is useful for future build-flag archaeology.
decomp_lessons: When changing global include search paths in `configure.py`, audit source files that rely on prefixed quoted includes like `"baselib/name.h"`; they may need to become local includes if the compiler working directory or include roots change.
Compiler working-directory flags such as `-cwd source` can interact with quoted include resolution, so include spelling and configure flags should be reviewed together.
For decomp build reproducibility, keep compiler flags minimal and intentional; broad warning flags or broad include roots can create noise or mask incorrect header layout assumptions.
naming_conventions: Baselib source files in `src/sysdolphin/baselib/` can include their same-directory headers with the bare local quoted form, e.g. `#include "fobj.h"` instead of `#include "baselib/fobj.h"`.
Existing external/system-style includes in the shown files remain angle-bracket includes, e.g. `<string.h>`, `<baselib/spline.h>`, `<placeholder.h>`, and `<dolphin/os.h>`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No direct assembly or function matching tactic was shown; this PR is limited to build flags and include resolution.
Disabling `-W all` may reduce non-matching-related compiler warning noise during decompilation work, but the diff does not provide evidence about matching changes.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that modify `configure.py` include path ordering or `-cwd source` behavior.
Search for remaining quoted includes using `"baselib/` that may depend on removed or changed include roots.
Check whether `-W all` was re-enabled, replaced, or discussed in later build-system PRs.
